nodes.name: Supabase Migration: Create Memory Tables
nodes.summary: Phase 5 migration that creates three Supabase tables (memory_entries, memory_projects, memory_conversations) with RLS policies and GIN-indexed tags for the J.A.R.V.I.S. persistent memory system. Includes bootstrap seed data for codebase knowledge, framework decisions, and initialized skills.

nodes.name: memory_entries
nodes.summary: Central memory store with 14 category types (user-profile, project, task, research, learning, decision, codebase, conversation, daily-log, automation, contact, tool, skill, note). Contains content, summary, source provenance, inference flags, confidence scores, tags array, and temporal metadata. Uses GIN index on tags for full-text search.

nodes.name: memory_projects
nodes.summary: Project-level context entries storing name, description, repo_url, status (active/paused/archived), and tags. All projects have timestamps for creation and last update.

nodes.name: memory_conversations
nodes.summary: Compressed conversation history store with summary text, message count, tracked model used, and creation timestamp. Used for context continuity across sessions.

nodes.name: MEMORY_SYSTEM.md
nodes.summary: Documentation for the J.A.R.V.I.S. memory system architecture, categories, and retrieval strategy.

nodes.name: @supabase/supabase-js
nodes.summary: Supabase client library for PostgreSQL database access, used as the persistent storage backend for memory_entries.

nodes.name: Memory Core (Phase 5)
nodes.summary: Phase 5 status: Local persistence, CRUD operations, provenance tracking, and relevance retrieval are complete.

nodes.name: .gitignore
nodes.summary: Git ignore rules excluding build outputs (.output, .vercel, .netlify, .wrangler, .svelte-kit, /build, /dist), secrets (.env files except examples), OS artifacts, and Vite timestamp files.

nodes.name: .mcp.json
nodes.summary: MCP server configuration for Svelte MCP (stdio transport via npx @sveltejs/mcp), providing list-sections, get-documentation, svelte-autofixer, and playground-link tools.

nodes.name: .npmrc
nodes.summary: NPM runtime config enforcing engine-strict to ensure Node version matches package.json engines field.

nodes.name: AGENTS.md
nodes.summary: Project configuration and Svelte MCP tool usage guide for Claude Code. Documents available tools: list-sections (discover docs), get-documentation (fetch sections), svelte-autofixer (analyze code), playground-link (generate shareable links).

nodes.name: CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: Master operating manual for Super J.A.R.V.I.S. Covers: project mission, phase history (0-10), hook system (PreToolUse), safety policy (levels 0-3), core philosophy (10 rules), graph-first/skill-first/local-first/model-agnostic policies, Svelte/SvelteKit conventions, file structure, design tokens, validation commands, documentation protocol, tool generation protocol, and permission levels.

nodes.name: GEMINI.md
nodes.summary: Gemini CLI configuration mirror of AGENTS.md. Documents Svelte MCP tools: list-sections, get-documentation, svelte-autofixer, playground-link.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Public project overview for Super J.A.R.V.I.S. Describes features: model-agnostic chat, persistent memory, tasks/notes/projects/calendar, research dossiers, skills, tools, automations, repository intelligence, learning, audit trail. Includes run instructions (npm install + dev), verification commands, safety model summary (levels 0-3), and original scaffold notes.

nodes.name: jsconfig.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript/JavaScript compiler config extending .svelte-kit/tsconfig.json. Sets forceConsistentCasingInFileNames, resolveJsonModule, skipLibCheck, sourceMap, NodeNext module resolution, and node types.

nodes.name: package-lock.json
nodes.summary: NPM lock file (lockfileVersion 3) pinning all transitive dependencies. Key deps: @supabase/supabase-js@2.110.0, svelte@5.56.1, @sveltejs/kit@2.69.1, vite@8.0.16, tailwindcss@4.3.2. 1075+ lines of resolved packages with integrity hashes.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Package manifest for 'aib' (v0.0.1). Dual-purpose: SvelteKit app + npm library (exports './' with types+svelte). 6 scripts (dev, build, preview, prepare, prepack, check, ai:validate). Dependencies: @supabase/supabase-js. Dev deps: svelte 5, SvelteKit 2, Tailwind CSS 4, lucide-svelte, svelte-check, vite 8.

nodes.name: svelte.config.js
nodes.summary: Svelte configuration: vitePreprocess() for Svelte 5 compiler, runes mode forced on all non-node_modules files via filename check, adapter-node adapter for Node.js deployment.

nodes.name: vite.config.js
nodes.summary: Vite build configuration with two plugins: @tailwindcss/vite for Tailwind CSS 4 processing and @sveltejs/kit/vite for SvelteKit integration.

nodes.name: .vscode/extensions.json
nodes.summary: VS Code workspace extension recommendations for Svelte and Tailwind CSS development.

nodes.name: .vscode/mcp.json
nodes.summary: MCP (Model Context Protocol) server configuration that launches the Svelte MCP via npx.

nodes.name: .vscode/settings.json
nodes.summary: VS Code workspace settings that associate .css files with the Tailwind CSS language mode.

nodes.name: AGENTS
nodes.summary: Defines 9 subagent roles (Architect, Svelte Engineer, Toolsmith, Memory Engineer, Model Engineer, Safety Reviewer, Validation Reviewer, Researcher, Product Designer) with responsible skills, pre-flight checks, and hard constraints per role.

nodes.name: commands-readme
nodes.summary: Documents that .claude/commands/ is unsupported; maps slash commands to Skill-equivalent actions in a reference table.

nodes.name: post-edit-checklist
nodes.summary: Post-edit fallback script that scans a changed file path and prints which docs/registries need updating based on path patterns, plus requires npm run build and ai:validate.

nodes.name: pre-command-guard
nodes.summary: Detects destructive shell patterns (rm -rf, DROP TABLE, git push --force, chmod 777, npm publish etc.) and install patterns (npm install, npx), exiting with error for destructives and warning for installs.

nodes.name: pre-search-reminder
nodes.summary: Lightweight pre-search hook that prints a reminder to consult REPOSITORY_MAP.md when any search tool (glob, grep, find, ls) is invoked.

nodes.name: secret-guard
nodes.summary: Scans file content via stdin or args for secret patterns (OpenAI keys, Slack tokens, GitHub tokens, Google API keys, AWS access keys, base64 secrets) and blocks writes with exit(1).

nodes.name: session-end-protocol
nodes.summary: Prints a session-end protocol template for documenting changes, skills used, graph status, validation results, and next steps at the end of each work session.

nodes.name: settings-local
nodes.summary: Enables the svelte MCP server globally via enabledMcpjsonServers and enableAllProjectMcpServers flags.

nodes.name: ai-researcher
nodes.summary: Skill for researching AI topics: searches current sources, synthesizes findings into digests stored in memory_research table, displays in Research dashboard widget with quality rules.

nodes.name: app-factory-engineer
nodes.summary: Skill for creating multi-page Svelte apps from templates: scaffolds routes, layouts, forms, components, connects to API endpoints with full CRUD experience and 13 planned apps listed.

nodes.name: automation-engineer
nodes.summary: Skill for creating schedulers, reminders, and background jobs with non-negotiable safety rules: visibility, pausability, editability, execution logs, and no external autonomous communications.

nodes.name: coding-agent-engineer
nodes.summary: Skill for project scanning, architecture summaries, refactor planning, test generation, and code review with 9 coding agent safety rules.

nodes.name: documentation-engineer
nodes.summary: Skill for maintaining architecture docs, registries, decisions, and self-improvement logs. Maintains 12 documents with update triggers. Includes self-improvement log entry template.

nodes.name: graphify-navigator
nodes.summary: Skill for repository graph intelligence navigation. Operates in DEGRADED_GRAPH_MODE using docs/REPOSITORY_MAP.md as fallback before any structural change.

nodes.name: memory-engineer
nodes.summary: Skill for designing persistent local memory: database schema, RLS policies, Supabase migrations, TypeScript types, CRUD API, and search implementation with standard column schema.

nodes.name: model-provider-engineer
nodes.summary: Skill for creating LLM provider adapters (Ollama, LM Studio, vLLM, OpenAI-compatible, Anthropic, Gemini, OpenRouter). Implements LLMProvider interface with chat, chatStream, embed, listModels, healthCheck.

nodes.name: safety-guardian
nodes.summary: Skill preventing destructive actions, secret leaks, unsafe shells, and unauthorized publishing. Defines 3 approval levels, protected paths checklist, destructive command detection patterns, and secret detection regex.

nodes.name: senior-ai-engineer-coach
nodes.summary: Skill for creating learning paths, exercises, projects, interview prep, and progress tracking. Covers foundation/intermediate/advanced skill maps, portfolio project ideas, and interview topics.

nodes.name: skill-generator
nodes.summary: Skill for creating and registering new skills. Defines SKILL.md schema with required sections, validates workflows are repeatable, and manages skill registry updates.

nodes.name: svelte-architect
nodes.summary: Skill for designing SvelteKit architecture: route groups, layouts, server actions, API endpoints, and state management with runes. Defines project-specific SvelteKit conventions.

nodes.name: svelte-ui-engineer
nodes.summary: Skill for building clean responsive Svelte 5 UI with shared components, design tokens, Lucide icons, and minimalistic neutral design. Includes non-negotiable design rules and svelte-autofixer integration.

nodes.name: tool-factory-engineer
nodes.summary: Skill for creating new local tools from user requests: specification, Zod schemas, Supabase data model, API endpoint, Svelte UI page with dangerous tool confirmation rule.

nodes.name: validation-engineer
nodes.summary: Skill for creating and running validation gates: npm run check, npm run build, ai:validate. Defines failure root-cause analysis process and common pattern-to-fix mapping table.

nodes.name: codex-config
nodes.summary: Configures the svelte MCP server for Codex via npx @sveltejs/mcp with -y flag.

nodes.name: cursor-mcp
nodes.summary: Configures the svelte MCP server for Cursor IDE via npx @sveltejs/mcp in a JSON-based MCP server registry.

nodes.name: .gemini/settings.json
nodes.summary: Gemini CLI MCP configuration — registers the Svelte MCP server for IDE integration.

nodes.name: .jarvis/workspace.json
nodes.summary: Runtime workspace data — contains tasks, events, conversations, model runs, audit trail, automations, memories, approvals, rollbacks, briefings, execution metrics, and improvement proposals.

nodes.name: .opencode/opencode.json
nodes.summary: OpenCode plugin configuration — loads the @sveltejs/opencode plugin.

nodes.name: .opencode/svelte.json
nodes.summary: OpenCode Svelte plugin configuration — references the Svelte.dev opencode schema.

nodes.name: validate.js
nodes.summary: J.A.R.V.I.S. AI Validation Suite — checks core docs exist, skills have SKILL.md, registry consistency, scans src/ for secret patterns, and verifies protected paths.

nodes.name: app.html
nodes.summary: SvelteKit HTML entry point — minimal shell with inline div container, favicon, and Svelte head/body placeholders.

nodes.name: hooks.server.ts
nodes.summary: SvelteKit server hook — starts the JARVIS automation scheduler on boot and passes through all requests.

nodes.name: index.js
nodes.summary: Barrel file — currently empty (no exports). Placeholder for public library API surface.

nodes.name: runtime.ts (automation)
nodes.summary: Automation scheduler runtime — calculates next run times, fires notifications, executes automation actions (task/note/notify), and runs a periodic tick loop.

nodes.name: runtime.ts (briefing)
nodes.summary: Briefing generator — filters open tasks and upcoming events for the next 24 hours, generates recommendations based on priorities, and persists the briefing.

nodes.name: store.ts (core)
nodes.summary: Local-first persistence layer — atomic file I/O with temp-rename pattern, transaction queue for serialized writes, entity CRUD (list/create/update/delete), snapshot reads, and audit recording.

nodes.name: types.ts (core)
nodes.summary: Core domain types — EntityKind union, BaseEntity, Task, Note, Project, CalendarEvent, ResearchItem, Automation, LearningItem, AuditEvent, ToolManifest, SkillManifest, LocalMemory, Approval, RollbackRecord, Conversation, ModelRun, Notification, Briefing, ExecutionMetric, ImprovementProposal, UpgradePlan, and JarvisData aggregate.

nodes.name: runtime.ts (evaluation)
nodes.summary: Evaluation engine — records execution metrics, generates improvement proposals from failure patterns, produces capability snapshots, and handles proposal approval/dismissal.

nodes.name: upgrades.ts (evaluation)
nodes.summary: Upgrade governance — creates upgrade plans from approved proposals, runs pre-change validation gates (npm run check, ai:validate), and verifies upgrades post-mutation.

nodes.name: runtime.ts (intelligence)
nodes.summary: Conversation intelligence — manages conversations (list/get/ensure/append/delete), extracts user preferences/goals from chat as memories, and records model runs with execution metrics.

nodes.name: config.ts (llm)
nodes.summary: LLM configuration factory — reads environment variables to build LLMConfig with base URLs, API keys, model defaults, and feature toggles.

nodes.name: errors.ts (llm)
nodes.summary: LLM error types — LLMError class with provider/code/retryable fields, isLLMError guard, and factory functions for offline/auth/rate-limit errors.

nodes.name: anthropic.ts (providers)
nodes.summary: Anthropic provider implementation — LLMProvider interface with chat, streaming, model listing, health checks, proper auth/rate-limit error handling.

nodes.name: ollama.ts (providers)
nodes.summary: Ollama provider implementation — local-first chat, streaming, model listing, health checks, and text embedding via /api/embeddings.

nodes.name: openai-compatible.ts (providers)
nodes.summary: OpenAI-compatible provider — shared base for any OpenAI-alike REST API (LM Studio, vLLM, llama.cpp server, OpenAI, OpenRouter). Supports chat, streaming, model listing, embeddings.

nodes.name: router.ts (llm)
nodes.summary: LLM router — singleton LLMRouter that registers Ollama, LM Studio, OpenAI (conditional), and Anthropic (conditional) providers. Picks best available provider per task type with local-first fallback.

nodes.name: types.ts (llm)
nodes.summary: LLM interface types — ChatMessage, ChatRequest, ChatResponse, ModelInfo, ProviderCapabilities, LLMProvider interface, ProviderHealth, LLMConfig, and TaskType union.

nodes.name: api.ts (memory)
nodes.summary: Memory CRUD API — create/list/get/update/delete/count/export with category filtering, word-based search, and sort order support.

nodes.name: relevance.ts (memory)
nodes.summary: Relevance ranking for memory retrieval — term extraction with synonyms, overlap scoring against query terms, recency decay weighting (1/(1+ageDays/30)), filters below 0.05 threshold.

nodes.name: +page.svelte
nodes.summary: App landing page — the default route that greets users entering Super J.A.R.V.I.S. before navigating to dashboard or chat.

nodes.name: POST /api/automations/run
nodes.summary: Triggers an automation run — accepts a target (task, note, or notify) and body payload to execute one-shot automation actions outside the scheduler loop.

nodes.name: POST /api/chat
nodes.summary: Main chat endpoint — routes messages through the LLM router to select best available provider (Ollama, OpenAI-compatible, Anthropic) and returns assistant responses. Complex due to model routing logic.

nodes.name: GET/DELETE /api/conversations
nodes.summary: Conversation management — lists all conversations or deletes them. Supports full conversation lifecycle via RESTful operations.

nodes.name: GET/POST /api/evaluation
nodes.summary: Self-improvement evaluation — lists past execution evaluations or records new metrics (success/failure patterns, timestamps, model performance).

nodes.name: GET /api/intelligence/briefing
nodes.summary: Generates a briefing of open tasks and upcoming events, filtered by priority and sorted chronologically for the next 24 hours.

nodes.name: POST /api/intelligence/coding
nodes.summary: Coding intelligence — accepts a coding task description and routes to the coding agent for repository-aware code generation or refactoring.

nodes.name: POST /api/intelligence/research
nodes.summary: Research intelligence — accepts a research topic and triggers the research agent to gather information, synthesize findings, and produce a dossier.

nodes.name: GET/PATCH/DELETE /api/memory/[id]
nodes.summary: Individual memory operations — retrieves a single memory by ID, updates it, or deletes it. Complete resource-level CRUD for the memory collection.

nodes.name: GET/POST /api/memory
nodes.summary: Memory collection operations — lists all memories with optional category filtering and word-based search, or creates a new memory entry.

nodes.name: GET /api/models
nodes.summary: Model listing — returns available LLM models from configured providers (Ollama, OpenAI-compatible, Anthropic) for client-side selection.

nodes.name: GET/PATCH/DELETE /api/notifications
nodes.summary: Notification management — lists pending notifications, marks them as read (PATCH), or dismisses them (DELETE).

nodes.name: GET /api/repository
nodes.summary: Repository intelligence — returns repository structure, file statistics, and analysis data for the coding and research agents.

nodes.name: GET/POST /api/safety
nodes.summary: Safety governance — lists current safety state (approvals, rollbacks) or requests new approval for a protected action.

nodes.name: GET/POST /api/skills
nodes.summary: Skill management — lists registered skills or registers a new skill with manifest metadata and execution specification.

nodes.name: GET/POST /api/tools
nodes.summary: Tool management — lists registered tools with manifests or registers a new tool (id, name, category, permissions, inputs/outputs).

nodes.name: GET/POST /api/upgrades
nodes.summary: Upgrade governance — lists pending upgrade plans from approved self-improvement proposals or submits a new plan for validation.

nodes.name: GET/POST/PATCH/DELETE /api/workspace
nodes.summary: Full workspace CRUD — complete REST resource for managing tasks, notes, projects, calendar events, and learning items. Supports all four HTTP verbs for full collection lifecycle.

nodes.name: layout.css
nodes.summary: Global CSS — imports Tailwind CSS v4 and @tailwindcss/typography. Provides base typography and utility classes for the entire application.

nodes.name: favicon.svg
nodes.summary: Application favicon — SVG icon used as the browser tab and bookmark icon for Super J.A.R.V.I.S.

layers.name: Infrastructure
layers.description: Build configuration, package management, IDE tooling, and static assets. This layer bootstraps the project — it defines how the app is built, deployed, and developed, but contains no domain logic.

layers.name: Foundation/Core
layers.description: Core types, global state stores, design tokens, and Supabase client instances. This layer is the contract between infrastructure and domain logic — it defines the shared language (types), runtime state container ($state stores), visual vocabulary (CSS custom properties), and data transport (Supabase clients) that every other layer depends on.

layers.name: LLM Provider Router
layers.description: Model-agnostic LLM adapter layer. Contains provider-specific implementations (Ollama, OpenAI-compatible, Anthropic) and a central router that dispatches requests without hardcoding any single model. Configuration is driven entirely by environment variables.

layers.name: JARVIS Engine
layers.description: Core JARVIS business logic. This is the central domain layer containing memory persistence, conversation intelligence, tool registry, skill system, safety policy enforcement, automation scheduler, self-evaluation analytics, and briefing composition engine. All layers above depend on Foundation; all layers below depend on this layer.

layers.name: API/Server Endpoints
layers.description: SvelteKit server-side API routes that expose JARVIS Engine capabilities over HTTP. Each endpoint wraps a JARVIS runtime module with request parsing, error handling, and response serialization. This layer is the network boundary between client UI and server logic.

layers.name: UI/Frontend
layers.description: SvelteKit client-side pages and layouts. Each page is a Svelte component that consumes API endpoints and JARVIS runtime modules to render interactive views for the user's workspace.

layers.name: Agent/Skill System
layers.description: Claude Code agent definitions, skill specifications, hook scripts, and session protocol files. This layer configures the AI coding assistant that operates this repository — it is external to the running application but governs how Claude interacts with it during development.

layers.name: Data/Storage
layers.description: Database schema migrations and local workspace state. Supabase provides the persistent SQL layer; workspace.json holds runtime configuration on disk.
